LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MICHAEL CHAMBERS, AND CARBONE, SMITH & KOYAMA'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO FEES AND MAY MAKE A SEPARATE MOTION OR SEEK FEES WITH THEIR COST BILL, BUT A REQUEST FOR A PARTICULAR AMOUNT OF FEES FOR THE FIRST TIME ON REPLY DOES NOT GIVE SUFFICIENT NOTICE. [SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING] COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM ADMITTED, COMPEL, AND FOR SANCTIONS

Appearances:
Franciscus Dylan Rosario (soltrinox@gmail.com) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona;
Tyler O'Connell of Porter Scott (916.929.1481, toconnell@porterscott.com) appearing via Zoom for defendants and opposing parties.

The court did not receive timely notification that the tentative ruling was contested and will not hear arguments. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion is denied. The court has granted defendants' anti-SLAPP motion, and discovery in this action has been stayed since defendants filed their notice of the anti-SLAPP motion. See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 425.16, subd. (g). In addition, the motion was served with insufficient notice. The Code required 16 court days plus two additional court days for electronic service. See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b); id., sec. 1010.6, subd. (a)(3)(B). The court awards reasonable sanctions of $200 to defendants. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: A
ppearances:
Franciscus Dylan Rosario, 650.488.7510 soltrinox@gmail.com Plaintiff, In Pro Per

Tyler J. O'Connell, Esq., PORTER / SCOTT 916.929.1481 toconnell@porterscott.com Attorney for Defendants, CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE; CARBONE, SMITH & KOYAMA; and MICHAEL R. CHAMBERS

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF FRANCISCUS ROSARIO's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Franciscus Dylan Rosario's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint (SAC) is denied.

Defendants CSAA Insurance Exchange; Michael R. Chambers; and Carbone, Smith, & Koyama filed a special motion to strike Rosario's first amended complaint (FAC) under California's anti-SLAPP statute on April 9, 2025. Rosario subsequently filed the instant motion for leave to file an SAC on April 21, 2026, and it was noticed for hearing before the anti-SLAPP motion was noticed for hearing.

Generally, a pending anti-SLAPP motion does not prevent a plaintiff from filing an amended complaint. (Dickinson v. Cosby (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 655, 678.) However, "[a] plaintiff ... may not seek to subvert or avoid a ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the challenged complaint ... in response to the motion." (Contreras v. Dowling (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 774, 791.) Courts generally permit amendment despite a pending anti-SLAPP motion when the amendments would not affect the outcome of the pending motion, such as adding a new defendant. (See, e.g., Dickinson, supra, 17 Cal.App.5th at 680.) Plaintiff's declaration does not attach the proposed SAC. However, it appears from his description that he seeks to add allegations concerning the same litigation conduct that the FAC concerns. Because the court has by companion order granted the anti-SLAPP motion and stricken the FAC, this motion is denied as moot.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
